title: Clemmensen Reduction – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Clemmensen reduction describing acid-mediated deoxygenation of aldehydes and ketones to alkanes using amalgamated zinc under strongly acidic conditions, together with mechanistic proposals involving organozinc/carbenoid or carbanion-like intermediates.
notes: Key concepts: Zn(Hg), concentrated HCl or dry hydrogen halides, carbonyl-to-methylene reduction, strongly acidic deoxygenation conditions.

title: Clemmensen Reduction – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for the Clemmensen reduction featuring reduction of acyl-substituted quinolones and pyranones, late-stage deoxygenation in pumiliotoxin C and lepadiformine syntheses, and ketone removal in polycyclic undecane derivative construction.
notes: Examples emphasize chemoselective carbonyl removal under Zn/HCl conditions in heterocycles and complex natural product settings.
